titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Raymond Philip Morris Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Brad C. Pohlman and Martha Lund Smalley

controlnote.p: This encoded findingaid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid prepared according to local divinity library descriptive practices.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 22:35

did.abstract: The papers document Morris's long professional career as head librarian of the Yale Divinity School Library and prominent leader in the field of theological librarianship. Raymond Philip Morris (1904-1990) was head librarian of the Yale Divinity School Library from 1932 to 1972.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

chronitemset.event: Born in Garnett, Kansas to Charles Elmer and Margaret Ann (Riley) Morris
Married Jean Louise Kelly (Children: Thelma Jean, Marcia Louise, Raymond Philip)
Assistant Professor of Religious Literature, Yale Divinity School

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Raymond Philip Morris Papers (RG 80), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Documentation of Morris's work with the American Theological Library Association is supplemented by YDSL Record Group No. 81, Records of the ATLA Library Development Project. Documentation related to the China Records Project and the Yale Divinity School Library is supplemented by YDSL Record Group No. 92, Archives of the Yale Divinity School Library (contact Divinity Special Collections staff for access: divinity.specialcoll@yale.edu). Files relating to the Divinity School are complemented by records in YDSL Record Group No. 53, Yale Divinity School Memorabilia. Materials on the Mott Biography Project are related to documentation of biographer C. Howard Hopkins in the John R. Mott Papers, YDSL Record Group No. 45.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These papers date primarily from 1955 to 1975 and document Morris's long professional career as head librarian of the Yale Divinity School Library and prominent leader in the field of theological librarianship. The materials in this collection were selected for inclusion by Morris.

Series I, Correspondence, includes five folders of family correspondence. The remainder is general correspondence with librarians, faculty members, funding agencies, and church leaders. Morris was a widely known advisor for theological libraries and this role is reflected in the correspondence. Additional correspondence related to specific projects and committees is contained in Series III.

Series II, Writings, contains articles, papers, sermons, and addresses by Morris. Most of Morris's writings dealt with theological education and libraries. Though not ordained, Morris was an active member of the Methodist Church and frequently gave sermons in area churches. Also of interest are the various tributes to Yale Divinity School faculty that Morris delivered at memorial services for them.
Series III includes representative documentation of Morris's involvement in various facets of theological education and librarianship.

Series IV, Biographical Material, provides a small glimpse into the personal life and warm personality of Raymond P. Morris. Of particular interest are five audio cassette tapes, which include reminiscences by Jean Morris. Circular letters from the Morrises, found in Series I, also provide biographical documentation.

did.unittitle: Bainton, Roland - re: beginning position as Librarian at YDS
M - P (includes Paul Minear and Liston Pope)
May, Mark (letter from Raymond Philip Morris re: taking position at YDS)
Michaelson, Robert (includes letter to Florence Mittendorff (Mrs. H. Richard Niebuhr))
Morris, Jean and Marcia to Raymond Philip Morris
Morris, Raymond Philip to Jean and family
Morris, Raymond Philip to Jean and family
Morris, Thelma - letters to family re: events honoring Raymond Philip Morris and reminiscences re: ATLA Board of Microtext
Union Theological Seminary (while Raymond Philip Morris was in the Philippines)
University of Notre Dame (Includes: Albert L. Schlitzer and James T. Burtchaell)
Weigle, Luther (re: position as Librarian at Yale Divinity School)
"Church, Its Orders and Sacraments according to the Anglicanism of John Wesley"
"Current trends in Religious Thought and Literature" for ALA Round Table
"Implications for the Renewal of the Church for Theological Libraries: A Protestant Appraisal"
Council for Theological Education - draft manuscript
A Preliminary and Tentative listing of Books of Christian Theological Institutions in South-East Asia compiled by Raymond Philip Morris
"Renovations in a Crowded Cloister" in The Pioneer
"Revised Standard Version of the New Testament"
"Some Impressions of the Libraries in Protestant Theological Institutions in Southeast Asia and their implications for the Christian Church"
"Study of the Library facilities of the Boston University School of Theology"
"Theological Librarianship as a Ministry" in Yale Divinity News
A Theological Book List by Raymond Philip Morris
A Theological Book List - "An exact reprint of the 1960 text with a revised and enlarged index" - Published for The Theological Education Fund
Review: Fellowship of Saints by T. Kepler
Review: Name of God by P. Crawford
Review: Dr. William's Library by Dr. Williams
Sermon fragments and miscellaneous sermon material
L. Weigle honorary degree by Standard Bible Committee
Episcopal Theological Seminary in the Southwest - commencement address
Episcopal Theological School, Massachusetts - convocation address
Silliman College, Philippines - Honors Day address and 'Librarianship as Ministry' talk
Paul Minear: Yale Divinity School Shaffer Lectures - introduction
Douglas Clyde Macintosh - reflections on the life of

"A Study of the Library Facilities of a Group of Representative Protestant Theological Seminaries in the United States and Canada" - submitted in partial fulfillment of the requirements for the degree of Master of Science in the School of Library Service, Columbia University
"A Preliminary and Tentative Listing of Books for the Libraries of Christian Theological Education in Southeastern Asia" - compiled for The Nanking Board of Founders
"A Summary 1961-1966" report by Raymond Philip Morris
Committee on Financial Assistance from Foundations
Accreditation for Earlham School of Religion
Correspondence to Catholic Seminary Librarians meeting
Correspondence to theological schools: Austin Presbyterian to Wesley Theological Seminary
Correspondence to Dr. Jesse H. Ziegler at American Association of Theological Schools (AATS)
Correspondence to Elmer J. O'Brien at American Association of Theological Schools (AATS)
Correspondence to United Theological College, Bengaluru (Bangalore), India
Funding from Sealantic Inc. (includes correspondence with Yorke Allen)
Negro Theological Education, Interdenominational Theological Center in Atlanta. Includes letter to H. Richard Niebuhr
Library Development Program - "Aids to a Theological Library - Selected Basic Reference Books and Periodicals"
Ecumenical Library Project, Evangelical Seminary of Puerto Rico
Institute for Ecumenical and Cultural Research
Conference on the formation and transmission of values in society
Executive committee minutes (includes report by Raymond Philip Morris)
Historical materials sent to Raymond Philip Morris relating to personnel problems
Newsletters, occasional papers and miscellaneous documentation
Report to libraries of St. Benedict and St. John, Minnesota
Consulting manual from Library Management and Building Consultants, Inc. Evanston, Illinois
Episcopal Theological Seminary of the Caribbean
St. Patrick's College and Seminary, Hartsdale, NY
United Missionary Library, Interchurch Center, NY
National Endowment for the Humanities: consulting work
National Endowment for the Humanities: consulting work
Meetings with Harvard, Union and Princeton librarians
Ronald E. Diener proposals and projects
World Council of Churches library consulting work

Nanking Theological Seminary Board of Founders project on theological libraries in Asia - Trip by Raymond Philip Morris to Asia, including Southeast Asia Theological Seminary Librarians Workshop in the Philippines
YMCA-National Council Committee on History and Library (includes Mott Biography material)
YMCA -National Council Committee on History and Library
Photo of Eva Kelly, mother of Jean Kelly Morris
Advertisement for home talent play - Raymond Philip Morris actor
Photos of Raymond Philip Morris and sketch of Raymond Philip Morris by Roland Bainton
Photos of Raymond Philip Morris in groups
Baker University commencement and alumni pamphlets
Clyde Morris will, letters, and photo. Also, newsclipping of Gussie L. Morris
Travel plans and itineraries. (includes unidentified photos)
News clippings and biographical sketches of Raymond Philip Morris
Commission on Arts in Connecticut - letters by Jean Morris to various CT and U.S. legislators
Service of remembrance at YDS and obituary from Yale Library Staff Newsletter
Five audio cassette tapes including reminiscences from Jean Morris
Scrapbook : Library staff past and present, presented May 19, 1972 to Raymond P. Morris, Librarian, Yale Divinity School, 1932-1972
Letterbook : A salute to Jean and Raymond P. Morris from members and friends of the American Theological Library Association
Letterbook : A tribute to Raymond P. Morris, presented to Raymond P. Morris by the Alumni Council of the Yale Divinity School
